Staff of the Observers  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Charges: 20
Skill: 2H Blunt Atk Delay: 35
DMG: 12 Dmg Bonus: 130
This item can be used in tradeskills.
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +5 INT: +5 MANA: +30
Effect: Sentinel (Any Slot, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 10.0 Size: LARGE
Class: CLR DRU SHM WIZ MAG ENC BST BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

▲ Page top
staff of obervers
QuoteReply
# Jul 01 2002 at 5:10 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591780476578
2 posts
ok heres the deal ladies and gents.

yes the lens still drops. 2 places..
1 inside re.. dont recomend going there. as it can be dangerous. and too many walls around you. also those anoying gobbys will attack you if you sit down to med

2 behind re theres a zone called gorge of king xrobb. or sumthing like that.(canever remember the name).. broxx sopawns there. BUT he is either a long time spawn. or has ph's..

i killed him in the gorge. since it is much easyer. and i am able to move around more. also because i am a druid. and most of my good spells that i need to kill him. i cannot use in RE.

what i did to spawn him is. sit back away from the evil eyes. med till fm. then kill both of them. they are fairly easy if you are up in the 30's

how i killed Borxx. this guy is a anoying ****.. dont wanna mess with him... first off. med to fm. then heres what i did.
1. snare him
2. run back and let his pet follow me backa little
3. root the pet.
4. run a while. with Borxx behind you. wanna get him as far away from the pet as possible. with thought time to at least hurt him bad before the root wears off
5. root borxx. run back a little. dd,dd,root,dd,dd,root,dd,dd,root keep doing that till he drops or root wears off his pet
6. by now he should be dead. or the pet should be attacking you.. if the pet is attacking you. root it. stand back and cast 1-2 expulse summoned. will take him right out.
7. finish off what evers left.
8. loot.
9. TADA!
10. go get drunk and jump off kelenith a couple o times

Windwolf Glasgow(morrel thule server)
a druid of the 37'th season
▲ Page top

__DEL__1593361901124
2 posts
Ok...so I'm a Barbarian and a bit thick-headed...I don't understand...can the staff be recharged or not? If only the "Tinkered Staff" can be recharged how do I know if I have a "Tinkered Staff" or not? Do you have to be a Tinker to combine the Mana Battery and the Staff in the box? <sigh> My head hurts...please help.

▲ Page top
Names
QuoteReply
# Oct 23 2001 at 10:37 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594570654847
63 posts
I am curious why some people have things like "Sage" or "Schoolar" or "Guro" after their name? Just wondering how you get it.
▲ Page top
RE: Names
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2002 at 6:37 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1593361608148
View User Forum
Scholar
44 posts
If you're talking about the titles on the message board, you have a ranking from Alakhazam's based on the average score of your posts. If you post something stupid or off-topic, people will often rate the post poor just to make the score filter skip it. If you post good helpful info, people will usually give it a high score. A default score is given to all your posts based on the average, so if someone posts too many idiotic things, sooner or later thier initial post score will be so low, that the post will be skipped by the filter before anyone is forced to read it :)
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 16 2002 at 1:21 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) They have those 'titles' - Baron, Sage, Master, etc, etc, etc... because that is a status marking on thier Alternate Advancement system.
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 21 2001 at 12:48 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post)
The Lenes still are droped for this quest
QuoteReply
# Jun 13 2001 at 8:29 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592246957221
14 posts
First and formost....the lens still drops here. With the changes made, there are only two Evil Eyes here. After the war Borxx died, but his two companions lived and where captured. They are very easy to find, just go to the Captured Evil Eye room, and there you will find are...guess what...two captured evil eyes. But watch out they charm and you have to widle down the goblens as well. I recommend having a large group just for that room. Not like the rest of Runnyeye after all the changes where mid 20's can roam the area in very small group, this particular spot (when fully spawned out) can be a challange. I also recommend getting some kind of Caster to keep your group from getting charmed. If you can do this, then the situation can be a little more under control and you don't have to worry about your tank or you beating up on your group.

In terms of whether this weapon is worth the risk, especially if you are in your 30's, I would have to say NO...at least for the Priest class. There are better weapons for this class, at a lesser cost to you. But on the other hand, if your in the pure caster class or in a priest class, and your not to concerned over the melee portion, then this is a 3 and a half star weapon for you, as long as you follow my advice, the room should be straight forward.

▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 04 2001 at 11:59 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) thanks every1.
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 04 2001 at 7:01 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) if the effect hasnt worked for you its because it is not a proc u just right click it
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 05 2001 at 12:01 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) thanks i am just not to EQ smart. :-) (joesam)
How tp use Mana Batteries!
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2001 at 11:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757372373
1 post
Alright, i see that most of youw ant to know hwo to use mana batteries.... being the tinker that i am... i know this.

Go to one of the pirate camps in ice clad ocean(velious only), and buy a CLASS 5 MANA BATTERY (ONLY CLASS 5) from a certain gnome. Alright, now, either go to buy a toolbox from a merchant in the steamfont windmills or from a guy on the pirate camp (dont know if they sell the toolboxes there, though).... Put the staff and your CLASS 5 MANA BATTERY in the toolbox and hit combine. Tah duh!....

(if class five dont work, try the others, but class five seems to be the only functional battery for most tinkered equipment.
▲ Page top
RE: How tp use Mana Batteries!
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2001 at 6:27 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591758476986
Scholar
62 posts
This is the correct way to recharge any tinkered Watchman Plate items. However, the staff is not tinkered - you'll get the "these 2 items..." message.

Markoff Chaney
Gnecro of the 24th Death

#Anonymous, Posted: May 22 2001 at 8:16 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) this is one stupid quest
sucks
QuoteReply
# May 21 2001 at 9:05 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
what kind of wrong minded chanter would want this staff.... when u can get a crude stein and like a rod of insidious glamour and get 27 charisma (?)
▲ Page top
RE: sucks
QuoteReply
# May 22 2001 at 2:06 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591756991154
4 posts
What kind of wrong minded enchanter would want 27 charisma, when they could get a decent weapon that adds a little to int and mana.

At lower levels, CHA doesn't do a damn thing for an enchanter, as you'll never find a group that wants to bother with assist and mez. At higher levels, CHA makes no difference because you get low resist mez and fixed charm spells.

So sure, theres a little use in the middle. Enjoy that 1-2% bonus you get from 27 charisma.
▲ Page top
RE: sucks
QuoteReply
# May 26 2001 at 5:39 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Sure, lower levels would want this, but I use a rod of insidous glamour and crude stein. I have over 180 charisma and at 35 could even mez stuff 40+. So dont tell me charismas not important when I see other high enchanters squandelling mana trying to mez the same thing. heres a thought though, what good does mana do when you cant land a mez?
▲ Page top
Function
QuoteReply
# May 05 2001 at 5:01 AM Rating: Excellent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I don't know if anyone pointed this out, I just skimmed over the posts, but the thing is this:

The sentinel does not follow you. You can leave the sent where you're standing, and go anywhere in the zone and you'll get messages from the original spot.

Unfortunately, if something spawns on it, and doesn't move, it give no message, so it useless for keeping track of most spawns.
▲ Page top
<Sigh>
QuoteReply
# May 01 2001 at 7:58 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591755048933
Scholar
127 posts
Well, I for one always hate logging on to this site and seeing some yahoo talk about how much a certain piece of gear or spell effect sucks, but...

I truly do not see the purpose of this spell. I find it somewhat annoying when someone casts it near me, but usually just move unless their doing it on purpose to spam people. Also, after level 35, this spell is almost completely useless, IMHO (but I am a Ranger, and we're not known for massive med times), as you no longer have to look at the book when Medding. Before level 35, this spell is really only usefull if you're soloing in a real unfriendly area. I, and I think any decent tank, always make it a point to watch out for monsters, which is the sole effect of this spell.

Anyways, this staff is great for casters, just don't expect to be making many friends if you're after it for the effect.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 19 2001 at 5:42 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) SDpel is nice and the most stupidest thing all new spawns come afetr you instantly in war room in hhk i casted and went to raider room to wait had a raider and by the time i ran i had 2 wars on me from the war room tried it a few times and if a group is attacking 1 then np but if any pop and group ignores it comes after you like a hot potato
